summary: Backend testing for Warehouse Workforce Management API. All 26 pytest tests passed (100%). Verified health, JWT auth (login success/failure, /me), self-register -> pending -> admin approval -> login flow, user list/PATCH, schedule auto-generation (Monday-only validation, 168+ entries for 12 active users x 14 days, employee 403), attendance self-marking with auto hours calc, leaves (emergency bypasses coverage check, annual with coverage block triggers 400 coverage_insufficient, approve updates schedule to type=leave, reject works, employee 403 on approve), and dashboard (admin block present for manager with pending_user_approvals/pending_leave_approvals/today_coverage, absent for employee).

minor.issue: Backend logs show: 'AttributeError: module bcrypt has no attribute __about__' during startup. Non-blocking (passlib falls back), but indicates passlib/bcrypt version mismatch. Consider pinning bcrypt<4 or upgrading passlib to a compatible build.

When no schedule exists for the requested date (e.g. far future like 2027), coverage counts are all 0 and the leave is rejected with coverage_insufficient. This may be confusing UX since technically there is no roster to violate. Consider: if zero schedules exist for the date, skip coverage check or surface a clearer message ('no schedule generated yet — please contact admin').

Emergency leave type has no balance field, so approving it does NOT decrement any balance. This is current behavior. Confirm this is intended (PRD says emergency type exists but balance handling not specified).

action_items: (Optional) Pin bcrypt<4 in requirements.txt to silence passlib version warning at startup.
(Optional) Improve coverage check to handle 'no schedule generated yet' case more gracefully.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is ~950 lines — borderline acceptable but consider splitting into modules (auth, users, schedule, attendance, leaves, dashboard) for maintainability as features grow.

Coverage check in _check_coverage counts only shifts in {morning, afternoon, night}; 12hr Saturday shifts (sat_day/sat_night) and Sunday shifts are NOT counted. This is acceptable per PRD which specifies coverage rule for weekday shifts, but should be documented.
approve_user does NOT validate that target user was 'pending' — calling approve on an active user silently sets it to active again. Minor, but consider explicit check.
update_user (PATCH) allows admin to change role/status freely with no audit log — acceptable for v1.
ScheduleEntry.id is regenerated on every upsert in generate_schedule, so the id is not stable across regenerations. Not a bug per spec but may surprise clients caching by id.
seed_data_creation: Test class registered one fresh test user with random UUID email (TEST_*) and approved it. No cleanup performed — these will remain in users collection but do not affect functional tests.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend is stable at 100% pass rate. Schedules for 2026-06-01 to 2026-06-14 are now seeded. Approved emergency leave for staff_a1 on 2026-06-03 is in DB (schedule entry for that date = type 'leave'). One TEST_* user was registered and approved per run, accumulating over time. For frontend testing, use Manager: manager@warehouse.com / Manager@123 and Staff: midhun@warehouse.com / Staff@123. EXPO_PUBLIC_BACKEND_URL is the correct env key (not EXPO_BACKEND_URL).
